channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Things to know about Russia's release of American Marc Fogel and the impact on ending Ukraine war
item.description: The U.S. and Russia agreed to a prisoner swap involving Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who the Biden administration had deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, in a diplomatic move that the White House said could move forward negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus releases 3 people, including an American and a jailed journalist
item.description: U.S. officials say Belarus has released three people, including an American and a journalist, who’d been detained for more than three years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leavitt defends banning AP from Oval Office over 'Gulf of America' stance
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt defended a decision by the White House on Tuesday to keep The Associated Press out of the pool of reporters allowed inside the Oval Office to cover an executive order signing with President Trump and billionaire Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: White House says American among three freed in Belarus
item.description: The White House said Wednesday that President Trump secured the release of at least three people jailed in Belarus, including an American it has not named and a Belarusian journalist.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel threatens 'all hell will break loose' on Hamas in latest Gaza ceasefire crisis
item.description: Israel’s defense minister says “all hell will break loose” if Hamas does not release three hostages as planned this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Protests after 2 sudden deaths force closure of hospital trauma unit in Central African Republic
item.description: BANGUI, Central African Republic (AP) — The sudden closure in the Central African Republic of the orthopedic trauma surgery unit at the capital 's main hospital following the deaths of two people, including a government minister's niece, has sparked concern among patients and relatives.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-FERNAND KOENA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrate the Lantern Festival with high hopes and rice dumplings
item.description: NEW TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrated the Lantern Festival on Wednesday by releasing paper lanterns into the night sky, visiting light installations and snacking on glutinous rice dumplings.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Too few tents entering Gaza threatens the truce. Here’s what’s happening
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Three weeks into the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, the number of tents and temporary homes entering Gaza risks falling short of the goals set for the deal's first phase.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Trump official Witkoff ‘not sure’ if Fogel exchanged for Russian prisoner
item.description: The Trump official who helped negotiate the release of an American held in prison in Russia for more than three years said in an interview with NewsNation that he wouldn’t comment on whether the U.S. released any Russian prisoners in exchange.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Austrian far-right leader's efforts to form a new government collapse
item.description: Austrian far-right leader Herbert Kickl says his talks on forming a coalition government with a conservative party have collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio: Marc Fogel release ‘good sign’ for cooperation with Putin end Ukraine war
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the release of American Marc Fogel from Russian captivity is a “good sign” for the ability of the U.S. and Russian President Vladimir Putin to work in cooperation to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Jailed Russian released in US in exchange for Marc Fogel: Kremlin
item.description: The Kremlin said early Wednesday that American Marc Fogel's release came in conjunction with the Trump administration granting freedom to a Russian citizen held in a U.S. prison — though they were not identified.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: UN rights office estimates up to 1,400 killed in crackdown on protests in Bangladesh
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. human rights office on Wednesday estimated that up to 1,400 people may have been killed in Bangladesh over three weeks last summer in a crackdown on student-led protests against the now-ousted former prime minister.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief suggests Ukraine should abandon hope of winning all territory back from Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says NATO membership for Ukraine is unrealistic and in sweeping remarks suggested Kyiv should abandon hopes of winning all its territory back from Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump upends US policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump has upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine, saying he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Egypt and Qatar are working to salvage the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: Egyptian and Qatari mediators are working to salvage the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas, which risks being derailed because of a dispute between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio says American Marc Fogel freed from Russia due to 'strength' of Trump
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday that Marc Fogel, an American who had been detained in Russia since 2021, was freed because the U.S. has a "strong president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump readies matching tariffs on trade partners, possibly setting up a major economic showdown
item.description: President Donald Trump could sign an order as soon as Wednesday to require that U.S. tariffs on imports match the tax rates charged by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US aid freeze sets back fight against human trafficking in Cambodia
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump’s freeze on foreign assistance has dealt a blow to organizations fighting human trafficking and forced labor in Cambodia, where tens of thousands of people are held captive and forced to work in call centers running telephone scams.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Families bid farewell to victims of Guatemala bus crash
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO LOS OCOTES, Guatemala (AP) — Hours before dawn, Julio Arrivillaga and Catalina Pérez Molina boarded a bus with other residents in the center of this humble village for what should have been an hour-long ride to Guatemala’s capital.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis accepts the resignation of a Peruvian bishop accused of ignoring abuse
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Pope Francis on Tuesday accepted the resignation of an influential Peruvian bishop, who has come under criticism for not doing enough to stop abuses committed by a conservative Catholic movement that was recently disbanded by the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: American Marc Fogel released from Russian custody
item.description: An American teacher detained by Russia is heading back to American soil, the Trump administration announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: French girl, 11, found dead near school, murder investigation underway
item.description: Investigators in France have launched a murder investigation after a missing 11-year-old girl was found dead in a wooded area near her school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video: Rare ‘Black Demon’ fish spotted alive for first time
item.description: The scary-looking fish is usually found in water depths of 1,500 meters, where little to no sunlight reaches.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas says it's delaying next hostage release, claiming ceasefire violations
item.description: Hamas is delaying the next release of Oct. 7 hostages, accusing Israel of violating the terms of their ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marine identified as victim of surveillance flight that crashed in Philippines
item.description: Military officials identified a U.S. Marine who died in a plane crash in the Philippines last week as Sgt. Jacob M. Durham, a California native.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare wildlife spotting caught on camera in Poland
item.description: A wildlife conservation group in Poland was conducting research on beavers when they spotted two rare black wolves on video footage captured in a forest last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defaced Holocaust mural finds new home in Rome's Shoah Museum
item.description: Contemporary pop artist aleXsandro Palombo is speaking out after his murals showing Holocaust survivors were defaced multiple times in Italy in apparent acts of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
